the_role_and_value_of_history_in_modern_.text: For many people, the past is more appealing than the present or the future. Discuss.
To what extent is knowledge of the past a good guide to future events?
To what extent is history based on lies rather than facts?
Nations and peoples should always forgive the wrongs of the past. Evaluate this view.
Studying local history is as important as world history. Discuss.
History is always written from the point of view of the powerful. Discuss.
‘The legacy of colonialism is alive today in many forms.’ Discuss.
‘Knowledge of historical events does not help us to plan for the future.’ To what extent do you agree?
The study of history focuses too much on what people have done wrong. Discuss.

wars_conflicts_and_terrorism.text: To what extent is the world a much safer place now than in the past?
‘Violence used for political reasons has always existed.’ Assess whether or not such violence is justified.
To what extent is military spending justifiable when so many people live in poverty?
War is only ever destructive and brings nothing good. Evaluate this view.
Countries with the largest armed forces always have an advantage. Evaluate this view.
Assess whether countries still need armed forces in order to remain powerful in today’s world.
To what extent should war always be the last option when countries attempt to resolve conflicts?
Everyone must help their country in times of war. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Examine whether or not the human race might benefit from the development of more technologically advanced weapons.
All young people in your country should spend time serving in the armed forces. Discuss.

the_state_and_its_institutions.text: To what extent can a nation’s written constitution safeguard people’s rights?

political_systems_leadership_nationalism.text: To what extent is it important for your country to have a good relationship with its neighbours?
A successful democracy needs the active involvement of its citizens. To what extent is this important in your country?
An effective leader needs to be a good listener. Evaluate this statement.
Evaluate the contribution of any politician or political party to the development of your country.
‘Unlimited power is likely to corrupt the minds of those who possess it.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘Election results are rarely predictable.’ Discuss.
To what extent do you agree that democracy is the most effective way of governing a society?
There are no great leaders in the world today. Discuss.
Assess whether a one-party system is an effective form of government.
Democracy is the best form of government. Evaluate this view.
Political leaders create fear rather than hope. Discuss.
‘Democracy is the ideal form of government but it is very difficult to achieve.’ Assess this statement.
Evaluate the qualities required for a successful government.
Voting in national and regional elections should be a legal requirement. Discuss.
To what extent does love of your country contribute to making it a stronger nation?
To what extent do flags unify or divide people?
Circumstances produce great leaders when they are needed. Discuss.
To what extent has government enabled your country to develop and prosper?
To what extent do the personal characteristics of politicians influence how people vote?
To what extent should people be proud of the nation they belong to?
To what extent do individuals have the power to influence how your country is governed?
Successful leaders have to make unpopular decisions. Discuss.

the_role_of_international_organisations.text: ‘International organisations, such as the World Health Organization and the International Monetary Fund, have become more important in recent years.’ To what extent do you agree?
International organisations are of essential importance in today’s world. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

the_provision_and_politics_of_aid.text: International aid never reaches those communities that are most in need. Discuss.
‘The provision of foreign aid by wealthier nations is motivated as much by self-interest as by a desire to help.’ Assess this view.
To what extent do richer nations have a responsibility to help the world’s poor?
Examine the impact of international aid on ending world poverty.
International aid benefits the people providing the aid more than the people receiving it. Discuss
Spending on foreign aid should be a priority for wealthy countries. Discuss.

justice_and_the_justice_system_prison_an.text: Modern technology has done little to prevent crime. Discuss.
‘Prison is ineffective in deterring criminal activity and rehabilitating offenders.’ Examine the possible alternatives to prison sentences.
Prisons play a positive role in keeping societies safe. Evaluate this view.
Evaluate the view that the aim of prison should be to improve people rather than punish them.
Evaluate the effectiveness of rehabilitation in the justice system of your country.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the treatment of criminals in your country.

the_role_of_the_individual_in_society.text: Assess the extent to which one person can change the course of events in society.
People achieve more success working in a group than on their own. Discuss.
To what extent is a person’s identity defined by the geography of where they live?
To what extent do individuals have control over their own futures?
To what extent is financial management the responsibility of the individual?
Assess the importance of everyone in a community being a good neighbour.
‘There is more to being a good citizen than showing respect and consideration for others.’ Discuss with reference to your country.
‘The modern world encourages self-interest rather than responsibility for others.’ Assess this view.
People should always base their actions on the opinion of experts. Discuss.
Improvements in society are more likely to be achieved by groups rather than individuals. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Financial security is the responsibility of the individual. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

family_marriage_and_partnerships_social_.text: To what extent are traditional attitudes delaying progress in your country?
The most important role of a parent is to teach their child moral values. Discuss.
To what extent has traditional family life lost its importance in your country?
Assess whether your country’s traditions are holding back its development.
Marriage is no longer necessary in modern society. Evaluate this view.
‘Families are less united than they have ever been.’ Discuss this statement with reference to family values in your country.
Evaluate how important it is for young people to spend more time with their families.

globalisation_and_its_impacts.text: To what extent has globalisation brought only benefits to your country?
To what extent has the world become a ‘global village’?
The world is more divided now than ever. Examine this statement.
To what extent has globalisation improved people’s lives in your country?

wealth_and_equality_in_society.text: ‘A government always acts in the interests of the wealthy.’ To what extent might this be true in your country?
The only way to achieve equality in society is for the rich to pay more tax. Discuss.
People do not need a lot of money to be happy. Evaluate this statement.
To what extent do you agree that it is possible to achieve equality of wealth in your country?
‘Wealth and happiness have nothing to do with income.’ Discuss.
To what extent would the even distribution of wealth bring social harmony to your country?
No nation can be truly happy unless all its citizens are equal. Discuss.
The desire to make a lot of money is having a positive effect on today’s world. Discuss.
To what extent is it desirable for all people in your country to earn the same amount of money?

population_and_migration.text: It is better to live in one location for your whole life. Discuss.
Discuss the ways that overpopulation is the most serious threat to the world today.
To what extent has migration harmed the development of your country?
To what extent is continued global population growth sustainable and desirable?
Human migration from one place to another brings many benefits. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Ageing populations bring many benefits to society. To what extent do you agree?

education.text: The main purpose of education is to enable people to get a good job. Discuss.
To what extent is achievement in education restricted by a student’s family and social circumstances?
Digital learning is the most effective way of educating young people. Discuss.
The management of personal finance should be a compulsory part of the school curriculum in your country. Discuss.
Evaluate the view that education does not encourage creativity.
It can never be justified to prevent a person from receiving an education. Discuss.
‘Education is the most important factor in increasing social and economic opportunities.’ To what extent do you agree?
To what extent should schools be allowed to decide their own curriculum?
To what extent should the ability to use Information Technology be education’s top priority?
The main purpose of education is to prepare people for the world of work. Discuss.
To what extent are timed examinations a fair means of assessment?
To what extent do schools in your country encourage creativity?
Learning is more effective when students travel to places outside the classroom. Discuss.
Online learning is the future of education. Discuss.
‘An uneducated society has never existed.’ Discuss.
Examine how young people can be inspired to become scientists and mathematicians in your country.
A country’s continued development depends on the quality of education it provides. Discuss.
In school, non-academic subjects are just as important as academic ones. Discuss.
To what extent is the main priority of education to teach people to read?
When preparing for a career, work experience is more important than academic study. Discuss.
Assess whether the teaching of speaking and listening skills should be given priority in schools.
Schools should encourage students to be competitive to prepare them for adulthood. Discuss.
Evaluate the importance of the arts such as painting, drama and music in education.

welfare.text: Assess the view that unpaid voluntary work benefits the participants more than it benefits the people the participants are trying to help.
A person’s welfare is the responsibility of their family. To what extent is this true in your country?
Evaluate the actions which could be taken in your country to improve the welfare of disadvantaged people.
To what extent are all disabled people provided for in your country?
Wealthy celebrities have a duty to give time and money to charitable causes. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent does the government provide effective public services to the people of your country?
Assess how effectively the government of your country looks after the welfare of its people.

sport_and_leisure.text: Evaluate the extent to which sport has become too competitive.
Sports personalities have a very positive influence on others. To what extent is this true?
International sporting events bring no benefits to the host countries. Discuss.
People participate in sporting activities mainly to improve their mental health. Discuss.
Evaluate the view that professional sport has become dangerously obsessed with money.
Evaluate the importance of public sports facilities for a society that is increasingly inactive.
‘The joy of sport is in taking part, rather than watching.’ To what extent do you agree?
In sport, individual skill and talent are more important than teamwork. Discuss.
Not enough is being done to ensure safety at sporting events. To what extent do you agree with this view?
‘Sport can unite people as well as divide them.’ Discuss.
‘Physical activities in schools are essential aspects of education.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘There should be more acknowledgement of women’s involvement in sport.’ To what extent do you agree?
To what extent does sport produce good role models?
Hobbies are a very important part of people’s lives. Discuss.
Men and women who play the same sport should receive equal pay. Discuss.
International sporting events no longer represent the true nature of sport. Discuss
Assess the view that sport should be about enjoyment rather than winning.
To what extent are disadvantaged people prevented from participating in sport?

work_and_employment.text: To what extent is it important for work to be enjoyable?
To what extent are workers’ rights respected in your country?
To what extent is working entirely from home preferable?
Evaluate the benefits and difficulties of working from home.
It is important to limit how long someone works each day. Discuss.
Essential workers are not appreciated enough in your country. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent do you agree that individuals should be free to choose their work or school hours?

industry_and_commerce.text: Assess whether it is possible, or desirable, to revive industries which have declined.
‘Engineers make a positive contribution to our lives today.’ To what extent do you agree?
A business’s main aim is to maximise profits. Discuss.
To what extent has online shopping improved people’s lives?
Assess the impact of unrestricted importing and exporting of goods and services between countries.
Evaluate the importance of the architecture of your country in understanding its history.
To what extent do you agree that rich corporations are now more powerful than governments?

freedom_of_speech_thought_and_action.text: People should be allowed to protest in any way they choose. Evaluate this statement.
The rights of people to protest on the streets should always be protected. Examine this statement.
Giving people complete freedom of speech can cause more harm than good. Discuss.
Protest groups have every right to adopt disruptive tactics. Examine this statement.
People should have the freedom to wear whatever clothes they choose. Discuss.

human_rights.text: The human rights of an individual should be given priority in society. Discuss.
Countries with poor human rights records should not be allowed to host international sporting events. Discuss.
The rights of minority groups should be supported in your country. Discuss.
‘The struggle for equal rights can never be won.’ Discuss.
To what extent is freedom of movement a fundamental human right?
Human rights are still not given enough importance in today’s world. Discuss.

animal_welfare.text: Evaluate the view that it is justifiable to use animals in scientific research.
‘Animals should be given the same rights as humans.’ Discuss.
‘In the production of food, animal welfare should be a higher priority than cost.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘The hunting of wild animals should be discouraged.’ Discuss.
The use of animals in sport can never be justified. Discuss.
To what extent should humans be responsible for the welfare of animals?
Animal welfare is as important as human rights in a civilised society. Discuss.
It is never justifiable to use animals for testing. Evaluate this statement.
To what extent is animal welfare everyone’s responsibility?

matters_of_conscience_faith_and_toleranc.text: To what extent is having faith in something or someone very important to the people of your country?
Strong religious belief can be both beneficial and damaging. Discuss.
To what extent is it important to be tolerant of the beliefs of others?
Assess the reasons for the decline of religious observance in some societies.
‘Religious belief in the existence of a creator cannot be based on an argument or scientific investigation.’ Discuss.
Assess the challenges for societies where there are many faiths.
Telling the truth is always the right thing to do. To what extent do you agree with this advice?

medical_and_scientific_advances_and_thei.text: Examine whether or not advances in scientific research have enabled humans to live healthier lives.
It is now possible for couples who cannot conceive naturally to have children. To what extent is this a positive development?
Examine whether there should be any limits to medical experimentation.
All countries are now better prepared to prevent the spread of disease. Evaluate this statement.
Clinical trials for new medications should always be ethical. Discuss.
‘The overuse of antibiotics has made them less effective.’ To what extent is this a serious threat to health?
‘Scientific research and development could eventually increase human life expectancy to 150 years.’ To what extent is this desirable in your country?
Examine whether there should be any limits to the advances of medical science.
Evaluate the extent to which recent scientific developments have improved people’s lives in your country.
To what extent has science made the lives of people easier?
To what extent has genetic engineering positively changed people’s lives?
Modified animal organs could soon be transplanted into humans. To what extent is this desirable?
Scientists can now successfully edit genes in human embryos. Assess the advantages and disadvantages of this.
‘Some developments in medicine appear to ignore ethical issues.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘Medical research should only be concerned with finding cures for the world’s deadliest diseases.’ To what extent do you agree?
The work of scientists should never be restricted. Evaluate this statement.
Science has caused more problems than it has solved. Discuss.
A country’s government should do everything possible to control the spread of infectious diseases. To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate the view that there is no place for creativity in scientific research.

drug_testing_manufacture_and_provision.text: Careful testing is the most important consideration when developing new medicines. Discuss.
Companies that manufacture medicines are only interested in profit. Discuss.
New medicines should always be fully tested before people use them. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

diet_health_education_and_provision_of_h.text: There is no longer any justification for humans to eat meat. Discuss.
‘Free medical care is a fundamental right of all citizens in every country.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘Inequality in life expectancy between the rich and poor is growing.’ To what extent is health the responsibility of the individual?
To what extent do you agree that an obsession with health is unhealthy?
Assess the advantages and disadvantages of free health care for all.
Evaluate how important it is for a person’s health, to spend time in the natural world.
It is every parent’s responsibility to ensure that their children have a healthy lifestyle. Discuss.
To what extent does a plant-based diet ensure a healthy lifestyle?
‘Without good health it is still possible to lead a fulfilling life.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘The main responsibility for health education lies with schools.’ To what extent do you agree?
It is possible to live a healthy lifestyle without being careful about what we eat. Discuss.
A careful diet is the most important part of a healthy life. Discuss.
Living a longer life is not always desirable. To what extent do you agree?
To what extent do online appointments with patients improve the way doctors provide care?
To what extent is health education effective in your country?
What an individual chooses to eat and drink is of no concern to anyone else. Evaluate this statement.
The government should protect the health of all citizens in your country. To what extent do you agree with this view?

space_exploration_and_its_associated_ind.text: To what extent has space exploration benefitted humankind?
‘It is difficult to justify spending money on space exploration while poverty exists.’ To what extent do you agree?
To what extent do you agree that space tourism is a waste of precious resources?
To what extent has satellite technology improved the lives of people in your country?
There are no benefits to space tourism. To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate the view that space exploration should be publicly funded.
Space travel and exploration should be managed by private companies rather than governments. Discuss this view.

information_and_communications_technolog.text: Evaluate whether playing video games is beneficial to people.
Evaluate the view that computer technology makes it much easier to learn another language.
To what extent has modern technology allowed people to spend their money more effectively?
The increasing use of social media is damaging people’s communication skills. Discuss.
Assess whether or not mobile phones have improved human communication.
‘Social media is now one of the main forms of communication.’ Assess the impact of this on society today.
To what extent might freedom increase if people turned off their mobile phones and computer screens?
Computer hacking is the greatest threat for the communication of information on the internet. Discuss.
‘In the digital age, we are more reliably informed than we have ever been.’ To what extent do you agree?
Using a telephone is the best way of communicating. To what extent do you agree?
Using a telephone is the best way of communicating. To what extent do you agree?
Examine the extent to which information and communications technology is having a negative impact on your country.
Genuine friendships can never be made only online. Discuss.
Mobile devices designed to connect people actually increase social isolation. Discuss.
Artificial intelligence should be welcomed not feared. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Evaluate the implications for individuals and society of a world that depends on information technology.
To what extent has increased internet use caused problems for law enforcement agencies?
Evaluate the necessity for online communications in running a business.
Technology can never fully replace people. To what extent do you agree?
To what extent do you agree that computer technology has decreased our quality of life?
To what extent have computer games had a positive impact on people?
Computer technology has made everyday life safer for everyone. To what extent do you agree?
To what extent should people fear developments in artificial intelligence (AI)?
To what extent does social media enable young people to communicate more positively?

surveillance_and_privacy.text: It is impossible to maintain privacy in an online environment. Discuss.
Evaluate the view that increasing surveillance threatens people’s privacy.
Internet activity can be easily monitored. Examine whether or not this gives any person or organisation the right to know what other people are doing online.
There should be no limits to a country’s surveillance of its citizens. Discuss.
Surveillance is only justified in the pursuit of criminals. Discuss.
Discuss whether an individual’s online activity should be monitored in the fight against crime and terrorism.
‘Facial recognition software and similar technologies may threaten the rights of individuals.’ To what extent do you agree?
Surveillance by the state threatens the rights of the individual citizen. To what extent is this true in your country?
Security cameras are essential to keep our towns and cities safe. To what extent do you agree?
People who obey the law have nothing to fear from surveillance. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
To what extent is surveillance in public places essential in your country?
It is unacceptable to monitor people using information obtained from their mobile devices. Discuss.

environmental_concerns.text: To what extent have human activities unnecessarily destroyed the natural environment of your country?
Assess how effectively the problem of household waste is being dealt with in your country.
Evaluate how successful your country has been in reducing pollution in its towns and cities.
To what extent has your country done enough to manage its environmental problems?
To what extent is it the responsibility of young people to protect the Earth’s environment?
The only way to reduce pollution is to regulate and penalise the companies and countries responsible. Discuss.
Not enough is being done in your country to tackle climate change. Discuss.
Assess whether or not sources of renewable energy offer a necessary and viable alternative to fossil fuels.
‘Not enough is being done to tackle the problems of waste disposal.’ To what extent is this the case in your country?
Assess whether it is important that areas of natural beauty should be preserved and protected in your country.
‘Our oceans are becoming choked with plastic debris, causing damage to all forms of life.’ Evaluate the possible solutions to this environmental issue.
Among all the other threats to humanity’s survival, the destruction of the forests is the greatest. To what extent is this true?
Fossil fuel extraction belongs in the past not the future. Evaluate this statement.
Exploration of the oceans is vital for the survival of the planet. Discuss.
To what extent is further development of renewable energy desirable in your country?
Trees have little importance in our increasingly developed world. Discuss.
To what extent can human societies learn valuable lessons from the natural world?
With reference to your country, evaluate which environmental issue should have the highest priority.
Evaluate the actions which could be taken to solve the most serious environmental problem in your country.
Evaluate what you consider to be the most significant future impacts of climate change.
As time passes, the world will end up with too much water. Examine this statement.
Humans should do everything possible to save all endangered species. Discuss.
The protection of the underwater environment should be the world’s most urgent environmental concern. Discuss.
People should reduce the amount of meat they eat to protect the environment. Discuss.
To what extent are the problems of water pollution being successfully addressed in your country?
The biggest concern about climate change is the threat that it creates for the people of the world. To what extent do you agree with this view?
To what extent should environmental impacts influence what people choose to wear?

natural_disasters_including_mitigation_a.text: Not enough is being done to prevent the loss of life caused by natural disasters. Examine this statement.
Human actions can prevent all natural disasters. Evaluate this view.
To what extent is it possible for governments to protect their people from the effects of natural disasters?
To what extent should more be done to prepare for possible natural disasters in your country?
To what extent is your country adequately prepared for natural disasters?
‘The human race is powerless when faced with natural disasters.’ Discuss.

rural_and_urban_concerns.text: The movement of people from the countryside to cities cannot be sustained. Discuss.
To what extent is migration from rural to urban areas beneficial to a country?
‘It is more desirable to live in a city than in a rural area.’ To what extent do you agree?
Examine the extent to which information and communications technology is having a negative impact on your country.
To what extent is the shortage of housing a problem in cities around the world?
All new house-building projects should include some green spaces within their plans. Evaluate this view.
Governments should prioritise the needs of people living in rural areas over the needs of people living in cities. Discuss.
To what extent should towns be designed to support people’s needs?
‘Beautiful spaces enhance personal well-being.’ Examine whether such spaces are valued and maintained in your country.
Identify the unique characteristics of a city in your country and explain the extent to which these characteristics are significant.
Parks and other green spaces in cities are unnecessary. Discuss.

food_and_water_security.text: Water presents more of a threat than a benefit to the future of the world. Discuss.
Only an increase in food production can ensure enough food is available to meet everyone’s needs. To what extent do you agree?
Water is becoming a major reason for conflict between nations. Discuss.
To what extent might it be possible to prevent future famine?
To what extent do you agree that people should avoid eating genetically modified foods?
The lack of clean water is a significant threat to human survival. Discuss.
The government of Qatar has laid down the following domestic guidelines: use leftovers, eat plants, cook at home, conserve water. Suggest whether or not your country should adopt such an approach.
‘Genetically modified food is the only solution to world hunger.’ Discuss.
Assess the issues which some countries have in achieving and maintaining an adequate food supply.
The problem of the global food shortage will never be solved. Discuss.
It is increasingly important that people have access to frozen fruit and vegetables. Discuss.

transport_travel_and_tourism.text: Assess the benefits of going on holiday in your own country, rather than travelling abroad.
To what extent is public transport an effective way to travel in your country?
Tourism always has a damaging effect on the environment. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Assess the most efficient ways of transporting people and goods in your country.
To what extent do you agree that tourists should not be encouraged to visit remote parts of the world?
To what extent should your country protect the environment when continuing to develop its transport system?
Evaluate the benefits of staying at home during your annual holiday, rather than visiting a foreign country.
To what extent is it necessary to reduce the number of vehicles on the roads of your country?
Evaluate the effectiveness of public transport provision in your country.
To discover and learn, it is necessary to travel. Evaluate this statement.
The problems caused by tourism are always greater than the benefits. Examine this statement.
To what extent is the introduction of electric vehicles a positive development?
To what extent are the problems of traffic congestion being effectively dealt with in your country?
To what extent does tourism make a positive contribution to your country’s economy?
The increased use of bicycles can provide an answer to the world’s environmental problems. To what extent do you agree?
Electric cars will be the most common form of road transport in the future. To what extent do you agree?
Assess whether travelling by aeroplane is the best form of travel.
Passengers should not have to pay for public transport in your country. Discuss.
Tourism ensures the survival of remote communities. To what extent is this true in your country?

the_uses_and_applications_of_mathematics.text: It is not necessary to study mathematics after primary school. Discuss.
Mathematics is fun. To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate how data and statistics can help people to make important choices in their lives.
Using statistical data is crucial when making decisions. Discuss.
‘Mathematics is the language of science and art.’ To what extent do you agree?
The use of data alone is not enough to ensure accurate decision-making. Discuss.
Assess the importance of mathematics in education.
‘Mathematical accuracy is unnecessary in everyday life.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘The modern world is obsessed with statistics.’ Assess the risks of only using statistics when making important decisions.
Evaluate the importance of mathematics in the world as you experience it.
To what extent has the use of mathematics positively changed the world we live in?
Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of mathematical modelling.
Evaluate the extent to which the use of mathematics is essential in the world today.
Mathematics has more importance in the classroom than in the outside world. Discuss.

literature.text: Literature should explore the less acceptable parts of human behaviour. Examine this statement.
A good book will challenge the reader's view of the world. Evaluate this claim.
Evaluate the extent to which works of fiction rely on the personal experiences of the author.
To what extent can children’s literature be enjoyed by adults?
To what extent do you agree that reading is the best way to acquire knowledge?
‘When power leads man towards arrogance, poetry reminds him of his limitations.’ (John F Kennedy) To what extent do you agree?
‘Monsters in films, fairy tales and books force us to imagine the worst about ourselves.’ Evaluate this statement with reference to at least two examples.
Identify an author from your country and justify the study of one or more of their works in schools.
Evaluate the effectiveness of film or literature in portraying the reality of your country.
Examine whether or not the reading of fiction enhances our understanding of human nature. You should refer to novels you have read.
Evaluate whether poets have any role to play in society.
Poetry is irrelevant in the modern world. To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate the importance of ‘the hero/heroine’ to readers of fiction books.
Assess the view that the main aim of children’s fiction is to encourage a love of reading.
Reading more books would have a positive impact on society. Discuss.
Stories written in the past still have relevance today. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Works of fiction have no relevance to the reality of everyday life. Evaluate this statement.
Examine whether or not the reading of fiction enhances our understanding of human nature. You should refer to novels you have read.
‘Film adaptations of books are rarely as successful as the original writing.’ To what extent do you agree?
Examine the view that it is the responsibility of art and literature to challenge society’s values.
Examine how the literature of your country influences people’s feelings and attitudes.
Films and live performances based on books can never replace the joy of reading. Discuss.
Assess the view that literature from the past has no relevance to modern life.
Wild creatures have a special appeal to the artistic imagination. Examine the basis for this statement with reference to any poems or novels you have read.
To what extent does literature reveal what it is to be human?
To what extent does a good story need to have believable characters?
Assess whether the novel or poetry is more effective in expressing how we experience life.
Evaluate the usefulness of science fiction in solving current problems.
Evaluate the extent to which literature can change how people think.
Books inspire the minds of children. To what extent do you agree with this claim?

non_fiction_writing.text: Evaluate the extent to which books about remarkable people or events can inspire their readers.
To what extent are works of non-fiction more important than fictional works?
‘Autobiographies are more reliable than biographies.’ To what extent do you agree?
Non-fiction books will never be as popular as fiction books. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

language.text: Evaluate whether having a global language would lead to a more united world.
The ability to communicate in more than one language is unnecessary. Discuss.
It is no longer necessary to learn to write by hand. Discuss.
In attempting to translate from one language into another, something will always be lost. How far do you agree?
Assess the importance of learning a second language.
To what extent do you agree that the continued use of minority languages should be encouraged?
To what extent are languages a barrier to world cooperation in your country?
English is the world’s most common language. To what extent is this desirable?
Non-verbal communication can be very effective. To what extent do you agree with this judgement?
Technological advances will make learning to speak other languages unnecessary. Evaluate to what extent this statement is true.

performing_arts.text: To what extent is music used for purposes other than entertainment in your country?
Evaluate the influence that the performing arts have on young people.
Music is better experienced with other people. Discuss.
Music has a significant impact on society and individuals. To what extent do you agree?
Participation in drama or other performing arts brings only benefits to young people. Discuss.
Evaluate the importance of live music to people in your country.
‘Music is a universal and unifying language.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘Going to a theatre to experience a live event is far more enjoyable and interesting than going to a cinema.’ To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate the importance of the live music and/or theatre experience for both performer and audience.
Music that is popular today is heavily influenced by the past. Discuss.
Governments should be responsible for funding a nation’s performing arts. Discuss.
Attending a live performance is the best way to experience music. Evaluate this statement.
Assess the extent to which music can influence a person’s life.
The best way to watch a movie is at home. Discuss.
To what extent does dance play an important role in the life of your country?
To what extent has technology had a negative impact on the music industry?
Musicians not only make music, but also make a significant contribution to society and culture. Discuss.
Actors playing leading roles in movies are too often chosen for their physical appearance. Discuss.
Discuss whether the performing arts are important to the culture of your country.
‘Melody is essential to music.’ Discuss this view with reference to music you know well.
‘The most memorable music is that which tells a story.’ Discuss.
‘This music captures the times we live in.’ Evaluate whether the music you listen to does this.
Evaluate the extent to which music reflects society.
Theatrical plays written before the twenty-first century have no relevance to life today. Discuss.
Involvement in performing arts, such as learning a musical instrument or performing in a play, can benefit people’s lives. Discuss.
Evaluate the view that horror movies no longer have the impact they once did.
Assess the view that comedy can never successfully deal with serious topics.
Examine the view that live theatre is not an accessible art form in your country.
The impact of music on people’s lives is always positive. Discuss.
To what extent has digital media negatively impacted attendance at live performances?
Songs have the power to divide as well as unite people. Discuss.

visual_arts.text: Creativity is only to be found in the various types of artistic expression. To what extent do you agree?
Nature is always presented positively in books, paintings and music. To what extent do you agree?
‘Sculpture has existed since early times.’ Evaluate the cultural significance of this art form.
Explain why artists, such as painters, filmmakers or musicians, are often inspired by terrible events in human history.
Illustrations and pictures are as important as the written word in communicating ideas. Discuss.
To what extent do visual arts play a vital role in today’s world?
Evaluate the view that the arts should not be about making money.
‘Fascination with the sea is common among writers, composers and artists.’ With reference to any forms of artistic expression, suggest why the sea might provide inspiration.
‘The most important quality of a work of art is its appeal to the audience.’ To what extent do you agree?
To what extent are pictures more powerful than words?
To what extent do you agree that films, literature or paintings can only reflect the real world?
Cartoons and animations have no serious purpose. To what extent do you agree?
Creative arts have no value other than as a leisure activity. Discuss.
Visual forms of art express emotions in ways that written words cannot achieve. Discuss.

applied_arts.text: Assess how successfully recycled materials are used for arts and crafts in your country.
‘Cookery is the only art form available to everyone.’ To what extent do you agree?
Examine whether objects that are used every day should be beautiful as well as useful.
The most successful businesses make the biggest profits. Discuss.
The main purpose of architecture is to add beauty to the environment. To what extent do you agree?
Food is a work of art not just a necessity. Discuss.
The usefulness of household appliances and furniture is more important than their appearance. Discuss.
Designers should always ensure that what they create is visually attractive. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

arts_institutions_and_venues.text: ‘Works of art are not just found in museums and galleries, but everywhere around us.’ Evaluate this statement.
Museums and/or art galleries are no longer required now we can view their contents online. Discuss.
Museums have no right to hold and exhibit items that belong to other countries. Discuss.
To what extent are art galleries and museums still necessary?
Government investment is essential for local community arts to survive. Discuss.
The main responsibility of museums is to educate people about their history. Discuss.

traditional_arts_and_crafts_heritage.text: Assess the extent to which traditional arts and crafts should be preserved in your country.
To what extent do you agree that all buildings of historical significance should be preserved in your country?
To what extent are traditional arts and crafts a vital part of your country’s heritage?
‘It is important for cultures to preserve their traditional beliefs and customs.’ To what extent do you agree?
Evaluate the importance of festivals and celebrations in uniting the people of your country.
‘National celebrations, rather than uniting people, can divide them.’ To what extent do you agree with reference to your country?
Assess whether your country’s traditions are holding back its development.
Evaluate the importance of traditional stories to the people of your country.
To what extent do traditional arts and crafts provide an understanding of your country’s history?
Examine how important it is that the production of traditional crafts continues in your country.
To what extent do you agree that the government of your country should give substantial financial support to the arts and crafts?
‘As the world becomes more of a global community, it is important to preserve traditional cultures in your country.’ To what extent do you agree?
To what extent does your country’s heritage restrict its development in the modern world?
To what extent is it important to keep traditional arts alive in your country?
Arts and crafts play no role in maintaining the heritage of your country. Evaluate this statement.
Traditional arts and crafts are not appreciated enough in your country. To what extent do you agree with this statement?

print_and_digital_media.text: The main purpose of films is to entertain. Evaluate this view.
Assess the view that print media is no longer needed in today’s world of digital devices.
To what extent does online news remove the need for printed newspapers?
To what extent is the media in your country biased?
‘News media cannot be trusted to report the truth.’ To what extent is this the case in your country?
To what extent has the communication of news been revolutionised in your country in the last 50 years?
To what extent do films have to be realistic to be enjoyable?
The media has become a dangerous part of daily life. To what extent do you agree?
To what extent do television programmes have a negative influence on people?
News media must always tell the truth. To what extent do you agree?
Television will always be the most popular form of entertainment. To what extent do you agree?
Digital media does more harm than good. Discuss.
Television has no educational benefits. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
‘Journalists should not interfere in the lives of celebrities.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘Blogs are the most reliable source of information.’ Discuss.
Newspapers in your country should not be allowed to publish the views of just one person or organisation. Discuss.
Printed newspapers, magazines, books and comics are no longer needed in our digital age. Discuss.
The digital book is never likely to replace the printed one. How far do you agree?
To what extent have streaming services benefited the film industry?
Television dramas about historical events are a good way to learn about the past. Discuss.
News should only be published or broadcast with the consent of those it is about. Discuss.
Understanding the target audience is the most important consideration for a journalist when writing for a newspaper or magazine. Discuss.

advertising.text: The popularity of a film or movie depends upon how it is publicised. Discuss.
To what extent should parents in your country be responsible for limiting the amount of advertising their children see?
Advertising is unnecessary if the product is good enough. Evaluate this claim.
To what extent can advertising be trusted?
Targeted online advertising is nothing more than an invasion of privacy. Discuss.
Advertising is losing its influence on people in the world today. To what extent do you agree?
Advertising is ineffective without visual images. To what extent do you agree?
Advertising has become an unwelcome feature of our lives. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
‘Television advertising rarely tells the truth.’ To what extent do you agree?
Stricter regulation is the only way to guarantee the truthfulness of advertisements. To what extent do you agree?
To what extent has advertising been improved by digital technology?
To what extent has society become too influenced by advertising?
To what extent is advertising a form of art?

censorship_and_freedom_of_the_media_and_.text: To what extent is it necessary to monitor the content of social media?
To what extent is censorship of films/movies necessary?
Evaluate the need for censorship in films and television.
To what extent should television and radio be independent of government control in your country?
Censorship of any form of artistic expression is unjustifiable. Discuss.
News that is free of censorship is the only way that people can find out the truth. Discuss.
The removal of works of art from public view is necessary when what they represent is now seen as unacceptable. Discuss.
Free speech should mean that news media is never censored. To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Discuss the view that the news media should never be censored in your country.
Effective censorship of the media is not possible today. Discuss.
‘Freedom of the media is important in a democracy.’ To what extent do you agree?
‘There should be no censorship of the arts.’ To what extent do you agree?
Censorship of the arts can never be justified. Discuss.
Any media with disturbing content should always begin with a clear warning for viewers. Discuss.
